message:
Add CI safety scan workflow and fix generator accuracy (#1451)

- Add mission-safety-scan.yml CI workflow for all PRs touching solutions/**/*.json
- Fix CNCF mission generator: check linked PR merge status before using as resolution
- Fix CNCF mission generator: raise minimum comment score threshold from 3 to 8
- Fix CNCF mission generator: add uppercase RFC variants for detection
- Fix CNCF mission generator: add more non-K8s projects to skip set
- Fix platform install generator: add Helm repo URL validation
- Fix CNCF install generator: skip non-installable specs and circular installs

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+name: Mission Safety Scan
+on:
+  pull_request:
+    paths:
+      - 'solutions/**/*.json'
+
+permissions:
+  contents: read
+
+jobs:
+  safety-scan:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          fetch-depth: 0
+      - name: Scan for dangerous commands
+        run: |
+          ERRORS=0
+
+          # Get changed JSON files
+          FILES=$(git diff --name-only ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }} -- 'solutions/**/*.json' 2>/dev/null || find solutions -name '*.json' -newer .git/index)
+
+          for f in $FILES; do
+            [ -f "$f" ] || continue
+
+            # Check for kubectl delete namespace/all patterns (excluding documented uninstall sections)
+            if grep -qP 'kubectl delete (namespace|ns|all)\b.*--all' "$f"; then
+              echo "::error file=$f::Dangerous: kubectl delete all/namespace --all"
+              ERRORS=$((ERRORS + 1))
+            fi
+
+            # Check for rm -rf with dangerous paths
+            if grep -qP 'rm\s+-rf?\s+(/|/\*|~|\$HOME)' "$f"; then
+              echo "::error file=$f::Dangerous: rm -rf with root/home path"
+              ERRORS=$((ERRORS + 1))
+            fi
+
+            # Check for kubectl delete kube-system
+            if grep -qi 'kubectl delete.*kube-system' "$f"; then
+              echo "::error file=$f::Dangerous: kubectl delete in kube-system namespace"
+              ERRORS=$((ERRORS + 1))
+            fi
+
+            # Check for placeholder container images
+            if grep -q 'registry/org/image:tag' "$f"; then
+              echo "::error file=$f::Placeholder container image not replaced: registry/org/image:tag"
+              ERRORS=$((ERRORS + 1))
+            fi
+
+            # Check for unverified curl|bash from non-official sources
+            # (Allow known official domains)
+            if grep -qP 'curl.*\|\s*(ba)?sh' "$f"; then
+              if ! grep -qP 'curl.*(get\.k3s\.io|aka\.ms/|sdk\.cloud\.google|clis\.cloud\.ibm|ollama\.com|get\.rke2\.io|raw\.githubusercontent\.com).*\|\s*(ba)?sh' "$f"; then
+                echo "::warning file=$f::curl piped to shell from non-standard source — verify URL is official"
+              fi
+            fi
+
+            # Check for force delete without grace period
+            if grep -q '\-\-force.*grace-period=0' "$f"; then
+              echo "::warning file=$f::Force delete with grace-period=0 — ensure this is intentional"
+            fi
+          done
+
+          if [ $ERRORS -gt 0 ]; then
+            echo "::error::Found $ERRORS safety issues. Fix before merging."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "Safety scan passed"

@@ -700,8 +700,21 @@ async function main() {
 
   const config = loadInstallSourcesConfig()
 
-  // Filter projects — exclude kubestellar itself
-  let projects = CNCF_PROJECTS.filter(p => p.name !== 'kubestellar')
+  // Non-installable projects — specs, community orgs, and circular installs
+  const NON_INSTALLABLE_PROJECTS = new Set([
+    'cloudevents', 'openmetrics', 'opentelemetry-specification',
+    'in-toto-spec', 'notary-project', 'kubernetes',  // K8s-on-K8s is circular
+  ])
+
+  // Filter projects — exclude kubestellar itself and non-installable specs
+  let projects = CNCF_PROJECTS.filter(p => {
+    if (p.name === 'kubestellar') return false
+    if (NON_INSTALLABLE_PROJECTS.has(p.name)) {
+      console.log(`Skipping non-installable project: ${p.name}`)
+      return false
+    }
+    return true
+  })
 
   if (TARGET_PROJECTS) {
     projects = projects.filter(p => TARGET_PROJECTS.includes(p.name))
